Call Now: 866.704.7692
FAQ
How can we help you?
Search

Celebrating 25 Years

of Addiction Treatment

Call 866 704 7692

Discover our Science-Backed Programs

Our addiction treatment programs in Florida allow us to target the specific needs of our patients to provide them with the support needed for long-term recovery.

Residential programs

A residential addiction treatment center like the one at Lakeview Health allows individuals to remain at the facility during their time in treatment, enabling them to fully immerse themselves in the program and focus on what matters most: achieving long-term recovery.

Learn More

Dual Diagnosis programs

Our dual diagnosis addiction treatment program addresses both addiction issues and mental health disorders simultaneously. When mental health disorders and past trauma are properly treated, addiction is less likely to be used as a coping mechanism.

Learn More

Male centric programs

At The Rose, our female-centric program allows patients to feel supported, understood and safe as they work through their recovery journey.

Learn more

Female centric programs

At The Rose, our female-centric program allows patients to feel supported, understood and safe as they work through their recovery journey.

Learn More

After Care programs

The primary goal is to assist in your immersion to society and continue your path of sobriety. Carefully, under the guidance of the Lakeview alumni staff and other alumni, you will have the advice and support needed to deal with everyday challenges.

Learn More

Experience the Lakeview difference

Our team of doctors, nurses, therapists, and counselors are committed to providing the most respectful, professional, research-backed alcohol and drug addiction treatment in the country. You’ll be in great hands as you take the steps needed to change your life.

Learn More
At Lakeview Health, you will be treated by an expert team of clinicians who understand what you are going through and will be there to support you every step along your recovery journey.

Get the help you need

When you’re ready to begin your road to recovery, turn to our team for the help you need today. Our admissions team is ready to help you determine the type of care you need.

Contact via email Call 866 704 7692
We accept most health insurances

We do everything we can to make sure the insurance verification process is simple and stress-free.Simply fill out our insurance verification request form or by calling us at 866 552 6557.

We accept most health insurances

Verify Insurance

Discover our addiction treatment center in Florida

Our facilities are designed with our patients in mind to ensure they receive professional care, while being immersed in a relaxing environment.

Take a facilities tour
Addiction treatment at a premier facility like Lakeview Health in Jacksonville, Florida can change the life of someone fighting an addiction to drugs or alcohol. 

Our Premier Centers

The Star

(Men’s rehab center)

The Rose

(Women’s rehab center)

Detox Center

Medical detox for substance abuse
Contact Us
Start your healing today!
Taking the first step towards recovery can be daunting, but knowing your options for care is paramount to your success.
Call now for a consultation
Call 866 704 7692

Lakeview Health

 Discover our Science-Backed Programs 

 
 

 
Our addiction treatment programs in Florida allow us to target the specific needs of our patients to provide them with the support needed for long-term recovery.

 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 
Residential programs

 
A residential addiction treatment center like the one at Lakeview Health allows individuals to remain at the facility during their time in treatment, enabling them to fully immerse themselves in the program and focus on what matters most: achieving long-term recovery.

Dual Diagnosis programs

 
Our dual diagnosis addiction treatment program addresses both addiction issues and mental health disorders simultaneously. When mental health disorders and past trauma are properly treated, addiction is less likely to be used as a coping mechanism.
